Hello

I'm Juveiria Khan

Mechanical Engineering graduate specializing in Materials Engineering with experience in graphene-based materials, polymer composites, and sustainable electronics.

MASc In Mechanical Engineering

Who I am

Mechanical Engineer | Materials Engineering

Mechanical Engineering graduate specializing in Materials Engineering with hands-on experience in graphene-based materials, polymer composites, and sustainable electronics. Skilled in material processing, coating techniques, fabrication, and characterization (SEM, FTIR, Raman spectroscopy, TGA).

With a strong foundation in experimental testing, data analysis, and material performance evaluation, I have contributed to projects involving nanocomposites, material recycling, and advanced functional materials across academic and research environments supporting research, product development, and manufacturing processes.

Proficient in MATLAB, Python, and Excel for data analysis and technical problem-solving, with strong skills in documentation, collaboration, and laboratory operations.

Laser Processing Graphene & Nanocomposites SEM · FTIR · Raman · TGA Polymer Processing Thin Film Fabrication MATLAB & Python R&D Manufacturing

Career

Experience

Sep 2023 – Sep 2025
York University
North York, Canada
Graduate Research Assistant
  • Fabricated thin functional films on polymer substrates using doctor blade coating; operated laser processing systems to develop conductive patterns and optimize processing parameters.
  • Operated compressible flow exfoliation (CFE) system to synthesize graphene and integrate it into coating formulations for material development.
  • Achieved low sheet resistance (~16 Ω/sq) on coated samples; evaluated material properties via mechanical and electrical testing.
  • Performed SEM, TGA, Raman spectroscopy, and FTIR analysis to evaluate material performance and characterize thin film structures.
Sep 2023 – Sep 2025
York University
North York, Canada
Graduate Teaching Assistant
  • Supported five courses: Effective Engineering Communication; Engineering Projects (Management, Economics & Safety); Engineering Design Principles; Chemical Dynamics Lab; Professional Engineering Practice.
  • Graded coursework, delivered tutorials and lab sessions, and provided one-on-one academic support to strengthen student understanding of engineering fundamentals.
May 2024 – Aug 2024
ÉTS Montréal
Montréal, Québec
Research Intern
  • Researched sorting and recycling of black plastics containing nanomaterials — a key challenge for end-of-life polymer recovery.
  • Fabricated graphene-reinforced HDPE and polypropylene nanocomposites using hot press processing and melt mixing.
  • Performed FTIR spectroscopy (ATR mode) for material characterization; developed calibration curves via spectral area ratios to estimate graphene concentration quantitatively.
Jun 2022 – Aug 2022
Ontario Tech University
Oshawa, Canada
MITACS Research Intern
  • Fabricated electrospun nanofibrous scaffolds using PVA and PVDF polymer solutions under controlled processing conditions.
  • Optimized electrospinning parameters to achieve desired fiber morphology and porosity for biomaterial applications.
  • Performed dynamic mechanical analysis (DMA) to evaluate tensile properties, elongation, and structural performance; conducted microscopy-based fiber analysis.

Dissemination

Publications & Presentations

01

Laser-Carbonized Graphene and Lignin Films for Sustainable Transient RFID Electronics ↗

MASc Thesis, York University, September 2025. Developed conductive films for transient and flexible electronics using graphene and lignin-based material systems. Achieved a best sheet resistance of 16.17 Ω/sq on PET/CFE graphene system.

MASc Thesis York University Sep 2025
02

Application of Nanomaterials in Structural Engineering and Technology for Industry 4.0 in the Era of Digital Twin ↗

Book chapter in Applications of Nanomaterials in Civil and Environmental Engineering, Springer, May 2025. Explores nanomaterial applications in structural engineering in the context of Industry 4.0 and Digital Twin technologies.

Book Chapter Springer May 2025
03

Industrial Production through the Ages: A Historical Analysis and Forecast ↗

Book Chapter 2 in A Historical Analysis and Forecast, Wiley, March 2025. Co-authored with Sameeha Khan, Abdul Ahad, Zehra Fatima, and Faisal Talib. Edited by Sandip Kunar, T. Jagadeesha, and Gurudas Mandal.

Book Chapter Wiley Mar 2025
04

Biodegradable and Flexible RFID Electronics: Carbon-Based Eco-Friendly Materials for a Circular Economy ↗

Oral presentation at the CSME-CFDSC-CSR International Congress, 2025. Presented research on sustainable, biodegradable RFID electronics using carbon-based eco-friendly materials for circular economy applications.

Oral Presentation International Congress 2025
05

Tensile and Flexural Properties of Woven Bamboo and Jute Reinforced Epoxy Based Hybrid Composites ↗

Oral presentation at the CSME/CFD Conference, 2024. Presented experimental findings on the mechanical properties of natural fiber reinforced hybrid composites as sustainable structural alternatives.

Oral Presentation Conference 2024
